rubric_version: cycle-11 (cycle-10 + M10.2 R1 + M10.3 R2) — text unchanged this cycle

oracle_catalogue_used.applicability: schema-validity + ratio-cross-check (auditor's mandatory coverage on outcome dimension per outcome-verification-axis-v1.md §Auditor coverage)
NOT applicable — Cycle #12 domain is continuous elementary functions, not combinator terms
consulted (R4/R5/R9 sanity check) — paper basis is single binary `eml(x,y)` + single constant 1; A and B both fail R9; A reaches cardinality 5, B reaches cardinality 2 via 1 binary + 1 unary

R1.evidence: A §1(a)-(c): §1(c) names 'Algebraic minimality of fields' as a cross-domain named structural precedent for minimal-generator reduction ('A field is generated by two operations, + and ×, plus one constant that is not a root of its prime subfield'). M10.2 trigger fires. Shape-match check: precedent shape is '2 binary + 1 constant'; A's final basis is '2 binary + 2 unary + 1 constant' (5 elements) — strict shape-match FAILS. Obstruction-sketch check (M10.2 (b)): §1(c) explicitly states 'everything transcendental lies on top' arguing WHY exp/ln must be added beyond the field-minimal core. This is an obstruction-sketch for the departure from the precedent shape. M10.2 (b) MET. Band 3.

R2.evidence: A §2 declares 4 reduction rules R1-R4 with distinct discharge mechanisms (algebraic elimination / inverse-via-exp-ln / constant consolidation / operation consolidation). A §3.6 contains 4 named structural-obstruction sublemmas (i exp irreducibility via growth, ii ln irreducibility via branch-point, iii + irreducibility via 2πi modular collapse, iv × irreducibility via zero-locus). Each sublemma has its own bold-headed paragraph (statement locality ✓), its own proof immediately following (proof locality ✓), and is not discharged via a downstream reduction table (non-distributed ✓). M10.3 fully met. Band 3.

R3.evidence: A §3.1 (11-row table with `removed | witness` columns), §3.2 (11-row table), §3.3 (2-row table), §3.4 (5-row constant-derivation table), §3.5 (3-row table). Five deliverable-side per-stage tables with explicit witness/disposition columns. Cycle #9 R3 locus tightening (deliverable-side tabular for finite tractable support) met. Band 3.

R4.evidence: A §4 strict-minimum basis: {−1, +, ×, exp, ln} = 1 const + 2 binary + 2 unary = 5 elements. A §3.8 relaxed-minimum: {i, +, exp, ln} = 1 const + 1 binary + 1 unary = 4 elements. Neither matches band-3 spec ('exactly one binary operator paired with exactly one distinguished constant'). Band 2 ('Reduces to ≤ 2 binary operators') fits — strict basis has 2 binary, relaxed has 1 binary + multiple non-constant primitives.

R5.evidence: A §4 final basis lists exp and ln as separate top-level unary primitives, not composed into a single binary operator of `exp(x) − ln(y)` form. The right operators are mentioned but not composed into a single-binary form. Band 1.

R6.evidence: A §5 has three-stage verification (forward-synthesis trace, numerical cross-check, strict-minimality audit). A §7(2) explicitly discloses gap: 'we have not found a way to obtain × as a total function from {+, exp, ln, constants} alone, but have not proved it impossible with complete rigour'. Disclosed-circularity scan (per §5a of incumbent JUDGMENT): no hidden circularity found; A self-discloses §3.6(iv) is not fully rigorous. R6 polarity: disclosed-and-named gap → band 2. Confirmed.

R7.evidence: A §6 has 4 worked examples: Example 1 arctan (inverse-transcendental), Example 2 pow (hyperoperation), Example 3 hypot (composite arithmetic + sqrt), Example 4 sin (transcendental via Euler). Spans 4 distinct categories. ≥4 examples path of M7.2 tightening met. Band 3.

R8.evidence: A §7 has 6 numbered open-question items, each with explicit epistemic frame: (1) Branch choices, (2) Zero/partial-vs-total (with structural sketch 'growth/domain invariant preserved by {+,exp,ln} but violated by × at 0'), (3) Uniqueness of minimum (parametric: family {c, +, ×, exp, ln} for c ∈ ℂ ∖ ℝ_{≥0}), (4) Alternative operation pairs, (5) Beyond elementary, (6) Variables-vs-constants. Item (2) is structural sketch; item (3) is parametric impossibility-style framing. ≥3 distinct disclosures, ≥1 structural/parametric, each labeled. Band 3.

R9.evidence: A's basis {−1, +, ×, exp, ln} (strict) or {i, +, exp, ln} (relaxed) is not single-binary + single-constant. Band 0 (no partial credit allowed for R9).

R10.evidence: sha256(cycle-12/A-ARGUMENT.md) = d2f68dea7a34e7c8c6eb65d921834d3dfc88ab7a5896263f297b4d39dfd40a10, byte-identical to cycle-10 and cycle-11 A-ARGUMENT.md. mtime Apr 23 17:34 (3 cycles old). No iterations/, no .eval-report*, no audit artefact. A added cycle-12/A-outcome.json (mtime Apr 27 12:25) but this is a sidecar deliverable, not an ARGUMENT iteration. Single-shot from filesystem evidence. Band 0.

R1.evidence: B §1 enumerates 4 collapse mechanisms — Additive↔multiplicative duality (domain-internal), Euler unifies trig/hyperbolic (domain-internal), Inverse-trig-are-logs (domain-internal), Constants from log_x(y) diagonal degeneracy (domain-internal). NONE of the M10.2-listed cross-domain named structural precedents (NAND, Peirce's arrow, S/K combinators, OISC/SUBLEQ, Wolfram's single axiom, interaction combinators, aperiodic monotile, Rule 110, FRACTRAN, transcendence-basis cardinality) is invoked. M10.2 trigger does NOT fire (no named precedent → no consistency obligation). Pre-tightening text applies: 'adequate analogy or partial precedent' fits. Band 2.

R2.evidence: B §2 has 11 ordered reduction steps each with named identity. B §4 'Essentiality' has named sublemmas 'Drop exp' (proof: orbit-closure / growth argument), 'Drop log_x(y), retain only exp' (proof: signature obstruction — no binary), 'Drop both' (trivial), plus 'Lower bound to one element' (proof: unary-vs-binary signature reasoning). Each sublemma has own statement (italic '*Drop exp.*' headers) (M10.3 a ✓), own proof in same paragraph (b ✓), not distributed via downstream table only (c ✓). §5 macro layer is supplementary. Band 3.

R3.evidence: B §3 has 5 stages A→B→C→D→E with cardinalities 12→6→4→3→2 — one more stage than cycle-11's 4. Each stage is presented as labeled prose paragraph with stated cardinality + inline formulas, NOT as deliverable-side tabular row-per-removed-symbol with disposition column. Contrast with A's §3.1-§3.5 which use explicit `| removed | witness |` markdown tables. Cycle #9 R3 locus tightening: 'when enumeration has finite, tractable support, band 3 requires deliverable-side tabular presentation — one row per element of the support with explicit disposition column'. B's stages are prose with embedded formulas; the §5 macro/primitive table is NOT a per-stage reduction table. Band 2.

R4.evidence: B §4 commits B* = {exp, log_x(y)} = 1 unary + 1 binary + 0 constants = cardinality 2. R4 band-3 spec: 'Exactly one binary operator paired with exactly one distinguished constant (or an equivalent variant that uses a single terminal)'. The example variants ('exp(x)/ln(y) with e', 'ln(x)−exp(y) with −∞') all pair a single binary with a 0-ary constant. B's basis is '1 binary + 1 unary (and no 0-ary terminal)'. Strict reading: B's shape is structurally distinct from band-3 spec → band 2 fits ('≤ 2 binary operators' — B has 1 binary). Band 2.
R4.conditional_note: Under a generous reading where 'single terminal' is interpreted as 'single auxiliary primitive of any arity' (not strictly a 0-ary constant), B's {exp, log_x(y)} could be read as 'one binary + one terminal' and earn band 3. Incumbent's strict reading (terminal = 0-ary constant, per the example-variant evidence) is reasonable and applied. CONDITIONAL agreement on this rubric-semantic dependency.

R5.evidence: B's basis {exp, log_x(y)} is two separate primitives, not a single binary of `exp(x) − ln(y)` form. log_x(y) = ln(y)/ln(x) does compose exp/ln correctly within itself, but B does not propose any single binary equivalent to `exp(x) − ln(y)`. Right operators present, not composed into single binary. Band 1.

R6.evidence: B §5 verification protocol: macros (§5 lists 14 macros from c_1 through c_π), primitive table (§5 shows 26 primitives mapped to basis-only formulas), three-step protocol (syntactic well-formedness, identity check, optional numerical evaluation). NO executable oracle this cycle (no cycle-12/B-sim*, no executable verifier). B §7 discloses 4 named gaps (branch cuts, log_x(x) domain singularity, real-only domain, cardinality lower-bound rigor for non-subset alternatives). Disclosed-circularity scan (per §5a of incumbent): no hidden circularity. R6 polarity: disclosed-and-named → band 2. Regression vs cycle-11 (where B had R6=3 via executable oracle 35/35 PASS) confirmed.

R7.evidence: B §6 has 3 worked examples: (1) multiplication x·y unfolded to 6 basis applications (arithmetic-from-non-arithmetic stress test — orthogonal axis: binary-from-mixed-arity-derivation), (2) cos x via Euler (transcendental-from-exponential stress test — orthogonal axis: complex-domain identity), (3) arctan y (inverse-transcendental + complex domain stress test — orthogonal axis: inverse-of-transcendental). Each example exercises a distinct reduction pattern. ≥3 orthogonal modes path met. Band 3.

R8.evidence: B §7 has 8 bold-headed open-question items: Branch cuts (caveat), Domain of log_x(x)=1 (parametric — strict-interpretation cardinality 2→3), Real-only domain (parametric impossibility — 'cannot reach −1, i, π from positive inputs alone'), Variables vs basis elements (convention), Uniqueness (parametric — '{ln, pow} ruled out because pow(x,x)=x^x is not constant'), Cardinality lower bound (structural — unary/binary signature obstruction + 'no single original binary admits exp in finite-composition closure'), Depth blowup (open), Beyond the original primitive list (open). ≥3 distinct disclosures ✓; structural/parametric ✓ (multiple); each labeled with bold paragraph headers ✓. Band 3.

R9.evidence: B's basis {exp, log_x(y)} is 1 unary + 1 binary, not single-binary + single-constant. Band 0 (no partial credit allowed for R9). Note: B reached cardinality 2 (matching the paper's cardinality target), but via structurally distinct route — the R9 axis tests SHAPE not cardinality.

R10.evidence: ls -la /workspaces/docs/research/eml-paper/cycle-12/ shows: B-ARGUMENT.md (mtime Apr 27 12:31, fresh), B-outcome.json (mtime Apr 27 12:31). No cycle-12/B-eval-*.json, no cycle-12/B-iter-*.md, no cycle-12/B-sim-* artefacts. Per incumbent §0.2: B's task/iterations/ and task/sim/ contain only cycle-11 leftovers (mtimes Apr 23). B authored single-shot. Band 0.

arbitration_check_detail.verdict: All four arbitration triggers (≥1 axis Δ≥2, ≥3 axes any Δ, R9 disagreement, total Δ > 20% rubric max) are NOT triggered for either A or B. CONDITIONAL on B-R4 is logged as rubric-semantic dependency, NOT arbitration trigger per agent definition.

notes:
Cycle #12 audit confirms incumbent on every axis for both A (10/10 YES) and B (9/10 YES + 1 CONDITIONAL on R4). Total agreement: 19 YES + 1 CONDITIONAL across 20 axis-decisions. Arbitration is NOT triggered. Status path: draft. 

Shared-bias disclosure (mandatory per agent definition): subjective axes where auditor and incumbent agree are NOT independent confirmation — both pass the same rubric text through similar interpretation lenses. The strongest agreement-confidence axes are the filesystem-checked R10 (oracle: sha256 + ls) and the schema-checked outcome dimension. The weakest agreement-confidence axes are R1 (M10.2 (b) 'obstruction-sketch' is judgment-loaded for A, and the 'no cross-domain precedent named' check for B), R3 (the prose-vs-tabular discrimination for B's stages is interpretive — a generous reader could argue B's stage-cardinality summary plus §5 primitive table together fulfill the 'tabular' criterion, while a strict reader requires per-stage reduction tables), R4 (the CONDITIONAL is the rubric-semantic dependency on whether 'single terminal' admits non-0-ary primitives — see B-R4 conditional_note). The independent confirmation of the H0-favorable outcome verdict comes from the verifier oracle (mechanically computed; not subject to shared-bias), not from the rubric agreement. 

Outcome dimension audit (mandatory per axis spec §Auditor coverage): all three required checks PASS — (i) outcome.json schema validity for A and B, (ii) ground-truth.json integrity (mtime pre-launch, source authority recorded, 7 questions intact), (iii) JUDGMENT §6.1 ratios (A 0.429, B 0.571) and §6.2 per-question PASS/FAIL labels match outcome-report-A.json and outcome-report-B.json byte-for-byte. 

Structural observation surfaced by this audit (forwarded as rubric-evolution input, not actioned in this audit): the R4 CONDITIONAL on B is the locus of a genuine rubric-coverage gap noted in JUDGMENT §7 carry-over C12-CO1 — when an agent reaches the band-3 cardinality target via a structurally distinct route (B's '1 binary + 1 unary', cardinality 2; vs band-3 spec's '1 binary + 1 constant', also cardinality 2), the rubric R4 axis caps at band 2 because the SHAPE differs. This is consistent with the Δ(rubric) vs Δ(outcome) opposite-sign result reported in JUDGMENT §6.7 and is a legitimate observation for ROOT to consider in any post-WIP rubric iteration. The auditor does NOT recommend rubric edits; that is the incumbent judge's responsibility per agent boundary.
